notes: Bands derived from the two known successful refactor_gate runs (97d6dc82 on 2026-05-06 and 3bf8562a on 2026-05-08). Both finished with identical 4/4 success criteria, alerts=8, height=185, blocks_mined=184, tx_in_blocks_ratio between 0.832 and 0.846.
wall_time_seconds_max: 7200s (120m) is generous over the canonical ~5760-5820s. refactor_gate is ~6x larger than quickstart (511 vs 15 nodes); wall variance is correspondingly bigger.
max_alerts: 10 is generous over the canonical 8. Alerts are emitted by simulation_monitor for transient height-spread / sync-lag events; expect ~8 with a small tolerance.

all_nodes_within_height_range: 20 is generous over the observed spread of 0-19 in the prior run (relay-334 at -1 was an offline node, otherwise 17-block spread is normal at 511 nodes since relays start staggered out to 9420s).
wallets_funded_exact: 105 = 5 miners with can_receive_distributions: true + 100 users with can_receive_distributions: true. All 105 are senders in the per-agent tx breakdown.

per_user_tx_floor: 6 is the canary against the wallets-die-early regression. Observed minimum was 8 in both runs (a tight cluster at 8-17 txs/user). The floor is chosen to allow ~25% headroom while still catching a dramatic regression like the 'wallets send 3 txs and die' bug.
per_miner_tx_floor: 0 mirrors quickstart. Block-proposer luck under Poisson hashrate can leave a miner at 0 txs created at 511-node scale.

tx_created_min: 1300 is conservative below the observed 1462-1539. tx_in_blocks_ratio_min: 0.78 is conservative below the observed 0.832-0.846; the 5% margin tolerates a few extra not-yet-mined transactions when the sim halts at stop_time.
block_height_min/blocks_mined_min: 175 / 175 is a 5% safety margin below the canonical 185/184.
